ground_truth.title: How to Find and Hire an SEO Expert in 5 Steps
ground_truth.author: WebFX
ground_truth.main_content: From plateaued traffic to website redesigns, SEO specialists provide the support and SEO expertise your business needs to generate traffic and revenue from search engine optimization. The three biggest indicators for when it's time to hire SEO experts are: Time, Results, and Resources. Additional motivations for hiring an SEO include getting support for critical company initiatives, troubleshooting sudden SEO challenges, and providing support while a team member is away. There are several perks to hiring an SEO expert, including improving your SEO performance, scaling your SEO program, adapting your SEO to economic, industry, or algorithm updates, aligning your SEO efforts with other digital marketing initiatives, and optimizing your day-to-day. An SEO expert, whether as a consultant, freelancer, strategist, or specialist, provides search engine optimization support to websites. This support can focus on on-page SEO, off-page SEO, technical SEO, or SEO content. To hire an SEO expert: 1) Outline your needs by determining how long you want to work with the SEO, what areas they'd work on, your pricing model preferences, and how you'll measure performance. 2) Set your budget, avoiding 'cheap' SEO offers as quality work requires appropriate investment. 3) Research options through your professional network, job sites like Upwork and Fiverr, social media sites like LinkedIn, review sites like Clutch and G2, and Google searches. 4) Find your SEO expert by reviewing their case studies, years in business, and public profiles. 5) Vet your options by meeting with candidates and asking about their industry experience, tech stack familiarity, and how they measure success. Signs of a good SEO specialist include setting realistic expectations about timeline (avoiding guarantees of fast results like 30 days) and providing full transparency about their business model and pricing. While you can do SEO by yourself, it requires time to build and maintain your knowledge. Google updates its algorithm frequently, so you need to keep a constant eye on industry news. If you don't have the time or resources to do that, consider hiring an SEO company.
